codeReview.summary: The fix improves packaging by adding `scripts/validate-milestone.sh` and a `services.yaml` `validate` command, but it still does not establish a guaranteed user-testing runtime finalization gate before success synthesis. Enforcement remains dependent on selecting a specific wrapper command rather than being intrinsically wired into the user-testing validator finalization path.

issues.description: The user-testing runtime contract still states the enforcement script "MUST be called" procedurally, not that finalization unconditionally performs the check. This does not prove integration into the user-testing validator execution/finalization path required by the feature.

The new runtime wrapper enforces flow reports only when this script is used, then runs scrutiny-style validators (`test-openburnbar-swift.sh`, `test-openburnbar-app.sh`, typecheck, lint). This is not a user-testing synthesis/finalization integration point by itself, so missing-flow enforcement can still be bypassed if the validator runtime continues invoking commands independently.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not clearly define which concrete validator runtime consumes `.factory/services.yaml` and whether `commands.validate` is mandatory for milestone validation, leaving enforcement semantics ambiguous.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `skillFeedback.suggestedChanges` explicitly notes uncertainty that orchestrator may run individual commands instead of `validate`; `.factory/services.yaml` now defines `commands.validate`, but `.factory/library/user-testing.md` still documents procedural invocation (`MUST be called`) rather than a canonical runtime hook (line 221).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature-to-skill assignment remains mismatched for infra validator-runtime work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission `features.json` assigns `skillName: switcher-integration-worker` to `misc-infra-integrate-flow-report-enforcement-into-validator-execution-path` even though implementation and review scope are validator infrastructure files (`.factory/services.yaml`, `.factory/library/*`, `scripts/validate-milestone.sh`).

summary: Review failed. The fix adds a new wrapper command and script, but does not demonstrate a non-bypassable user-testing finalization integration. The original blocking concern (runtime-path guarantee vs procedural invocation) is therefore not fully resolved.
